description:
# .43.F — wire lower_case onto matrix (same shape as .43.D, simpler callback)

## What changes

`lower_case` (src/ir_lower.rs ~2252):
- Same try_matrix_dispatch gate as .D
- Callback is SIMPLER than D's: case body is ALWAYS cont-fn-wrapped
  (no inline-body fast path), so no body_might_cps_split check
- Cascade fallback preserved for ineligible cases

## What stays

- Subject lowering + parking (today)
- join_opt (cont fn for non-tail) — handled by callback
- Per-clause cont_fn (mint at leaf, after env rebind)
- All semantics

## What's removed

- Per-clause try_blocks cascade for ELIGIBLE case-expressions
- Cross-clause duplicate dispatch tests

## Acceptance

- case_tuple_pattern_sequential's golden: byte-identical OR strictly
  smaller (depending on whether its clauses share constructors)
- All other case-using fixtures: golden byte-identical
- Three-path parity
- Existing lower_case_uses_per_clause_cont_fns test passes unchanged

## Risk

case currently errors on guards (line 2315). v1 keeps that — guard
clauses fall back to cascade. Future ticket: matrix-handle case guards.
title: fz-ul4.43.F: wire lower_case onto matrix
